jokes.text: Don't worry. She's dead. Oh, wait... He's dead.
jokes.setup: Dwight brings what appears to be a dead goose to the office
jokes.punchline: His casual correction about the goose's gender as if that's the main concern
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight absurdity - treating gender identification as more important than bringing roadkill to work

jokes.text: It's a Christmas miracle.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining he accidentally ran over the goose
jokes.punchline: Calling accidentally killing an animal a 'Christmas miracle'
jokes.explanation: Dark irony delivered with Dwight's characteristic sincerity

jokes.text: And circle gets the square.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what they have, Jim says 'dead goose'
jokes.punchline: Jim's game show reference treating the obvious answer like a game show win
jokes.explanation: Jim's dry wit in an absurd situation, referencing Hollywood Squares

jokes.text: Once, I brought in a duck to prepare for lunch. And people got upset. Apparently, they got attached to the duck and didn't want to see it killed. He was already dead.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his past office food incidents
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that coworkers got 'attached' to a duck he planned to butcher, and his confused clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - missing the point that bringing dead animals to work is inappropriate regardless

jokes.text: Thus saving you a trip to the store for a can of expensive goose grease.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining practical uses for goose parts
jokes.punchline: The implication that canned goose grease is a common grocery store item
jokes.explanation: Dwight's bizarre practical wisdom, treating specialty items as everyday necessities

jokes.text: Wow, win-win.
jokes.setup: Dwight's earnest explanation about goose grease savings
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic agreement using corporate buzzword for roadkill economics
jokes.explanation: Jim's perfect deadpan response, treating Dwight's absurdity as legitimate business advice

jokes.text: Clean it in your car.
jokes.setup: Extended argument about the dead goose in the office
jokes.punchline: Toby's defeated compromise that still allows the disgusting activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby moment - his powerless authority resulting in an even worse solution

jokes.text: Oh, Pam. Take a chill pill.
jokes.setup: Pam asking Michael to remove the dead goose from her desk
jokes.punchline: Michael's dismissive response with outdated slang to a reasonable request
jokes.explanation: Michael's tone-deaf response, using dated slang inappropriately

jokes.text: I would like a nice slice of Christmas Pam. Side of candy Pams. And perhaps... some Pam chops.
jokes.setup: Michael approaching Pam's desk during Christmas season
jokes.punchline: Elaborate food puns using Pam's name in increasingly uncomfortable ways
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael wordplay that goes on too long and gets increasingly awkward

jokes.text: Well, that is probably from the test drive.
jokes.setup: Pam noting the worn tires on Michael's 'donated' bike
jokes.punchline: Michael's transparent lie about test driving a clearly used bike
jokes.explanation: Michael's pathetic attempt to cover up donating his old bike as new

jokes.text: Yo, Michael. Sweet ride, Mike.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to pass off his old bike as a generous donation
jokes.punchline: Ryan's sarcastic compliment on the obviously beat-up bike
jokes.explanation: Ryan's dry observation highlighting Michael's transparent cheapness

jokes.text: Oh, you're such a blonde.
jokes.setup: Carol arrives early to the Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Michael's casually sexist comment disguised as affection
jokes.explanation: Cringey but character-appropriate casual sexism from Michael

jokes.text: This is just the front of her. Show 'em... show 'em the other side.
jokes.setup: Michael introducing Carol to the office
jokes.punchline: Treating his girlfriend like an object to be displayed for coworkers
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael cringe - objectifying his girlfriend while trying to show her off

jokes.text: It's a picture of you and me and your kids on a ski trip, having a blast. 'Ski-son's Greetings.'
jokes.setup: Michael showing Carol his Christmas card
jokes.punchline: The reveal that he photoshopped himself into a family photo plus terrible pun
jokes.explanation: Deeply uncomfortable Michael behavior - photoshopping himself into Carol's family, plus awful pun

jokes.text: Yes, but what you didn't realize at the time was that I was with you, in a sense. I was in your heart.
jokes.setup: Carol pointing out they never went on this ski trip together
jokes.punchline: Michael's delusional justification for the photoshop using romantic metaphors
jokes.explanation: Michael's romantic delusion taken to absurd extremes to justify creepy behavior

jokes.text: It's a bold move to Photoshop yourself into a picture with your girlfriend and her kids on a ski trip with their real father. But then again, Michael's a bold guy. Is bold the right word?
jokes.setup: Jim's talking head about Michael's Christmas card
jokes.punchline: The understatement of calling it 'bold' then questioning his own word choice
jokes.explanation: Jim's perfect talking head - diplomatic understatement that highlights how insane Michael's behavior is

jokes.text: You need... to think this through in... Jamaica's largest freshwater pool.
jokes.setup: Carol trying to break up with Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's desperate pivot to vacation planning as relationship solution
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional belief that a vacation will fix their relationship issues

jokes.text: For the past few months, I've been sending Dwight letters from the CIA.
jokes.setup: Pam giving Jim a Christmas gift
jokes.punchline: The reveal of an elaborate months-long prank on Dwight
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for extended prank reveal, showing Pam's hidden mischievous side

jokes.text: Last year, my boss, Michael Scott, took a day off, 'cause he said he had pneumonia, but really, he was leaving early to go to magic camp.
jokes.setup: Reading Dwight's list of secrets he'd never tell
jokes.punchline: The specific embarrassing secret about Michael's magic camp lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - both Michael's pathetic lie and Dwight's earnest record-keeping

jokes.text: Sorry I didn't wrap it.
jokes.setup: Pam finishing explanation of elaborate CIA prank gift
jokes.punchline: Casual apology for not wrapping months of deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan ending to the prank reveal - treating gift wrapping as the oversight

jokes.text: Christmas is canceled.
jokes.setup: Michael returning from his breakup with Carol
jokes.punchline: Declaring he can cancel a federal holiday due to personal problems
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael narcissism - believing his personal issues affect national holidays

jokes.text: Keep it up, Stanley, and you will lose New Year's.
jokes.setup: Stanley pointing out you can't cancel holidays
jokes.punchline: Michael doubling down with threat to cancel more holidays
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd premise, showing Michael's commitment to his delusion

jokes.text: Jim, take New Year's away from Stanley!
jokes.setup: Michael's threat to cancel New Year's for Stanley
jokes.punchline: Ordering Jim to somehow enforce this impossible punishment
jokes.explanation: Further escalation showing Michael's disconnect from reality and Jim's unwilling involvement

jokes.text: All of your guests would have probably canceled at the last minute anyway. Leaving your life a stupid, rotten mess.
jokes.setup: Phyllis defending the Christmas party
jokes.punchline: Michael projecting his own situation onto Phyllis with cruel specificity
jokes.explanation: Dark Michael moment - taking out his pain on innocent Phyllis with oddly specific cruelty

jokes.text: It hurts my heart. It hurts my stomach. Hurts my arms.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his heartbreak to Jim
jokes.punchline: Physical pain expanding to increasingly random body parts
jokes.explanation: Michael's dramatic overstatement of heartbreak with absurd physical manifestations

jokes.text: Like maybe the real reason they left was because... there were things they wanted you to do in bed... that were... foreign and scary.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Jim how to push away bad thoughts
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately sharing his most inappropriate insecurities
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael TMI moment - oversharing intimate fears to unwilling Jim

jokes.text: Take it from Toby.
jokes.setup: Dwight reporting they're short one bathrobe
jokes.punchline: Michael's instant solution to take from his least favorite employee
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael solution - solve problems by victimizing Toby

jokes.text: Pam, would you like to go to Sandals, Jamaica with me?
jokes.setup: Michael dealing with his canceled romantic vacation
jokes.punchline: Casually asking his receptionist on the romantic getaway meant for Carol
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate rebound desperation, treating Pam like a replacement date

jokes.text: I thought you said green was whorish. No, orange is whorish.
jokes.setup: Party planning discussion about decorations
jokes.punchline: Angela's matter-of-fact color morality system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Angela character moment - her rigid moral judgments applied to arbitrary things like colors

jokes.text: You tried this out. And it's clearly not for you. It's time to go.
jokes.setup: Karen suggesting party ideas to Angela
jokes.punchline: Angela firing her from voluntary party planning like it's a job
jokes.explanation: Peak Angela control freak behavior - treating volunteer party help like employment

jokes.text: That Carol is one smart cookie.
jokes.setup: Michael and Dwight going through items that remind him of Carol
jokes.punchline: Michael praising Carol immediately after trying to forget her
jokes.explanation: Michael's inability to actually let go of Carol, undermining his own therapy exercise

jokes.text: With Carol, I knew within the first 24 minutes of the second day I met her.
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about knowing true love within 24 hours
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and delayed timeline that contradicts the adage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - trying to sound wise but revealing his own confusion and overthinking

jokes.text: I think one of her cats did once. She came in with scratches all over her face.
jokes.setup: Karen asking if anyone ever stands up to Angela
jokes.punchline: The implication that Angela's cats are the only ones brave enough to fight back
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor building on Angela's cat lady persona with dark implication

jokes.text: The Committee to Plan Parties invites you to a Margarita-Karaoke Christmas.
jokes.setup: Pam and Karen starting rival party
jokes.punchline: The formal committee name for their rebellion against Angela
jokes.explanation: Good setup for office politics comedy with bureaucratic party planning warfare

jokes.text: What's your funding? $200. What's ours again? Um, $201.
jokes.setup: Angela questioning the new committee's legitimacy
jokes.punchline: The petty one-dollar funding war
jokes.explanation: Perfect petty office rivalry escalation with specific dollar amounts making it more absurd

jokes.text: And then there's a way more fun party that starts at 2:45.
jokes.setup: Explaining the two competing parties
jokes.punchline: Starting 15 minutes earlier to gain competitive advantage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of petty competition with specific time-based warfare

jokes.text: As ranking number three in this office... Um, eh... I'm number three. You're number four.
jokes.setup: Angela trying to assert authority over party planning
jokes.punchline: Immediate challenge to her ranking with office hierarchy debate
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing office power dynamics and Dwight's competitive nature

jokes.text: As ranking number two, I am starting a committee to determine the validity of the two committees. And I am the sole member.
jokes.setup: Office hierarchy debate over party planning authority
jokes.punchline: Jim's bureaucratic solution creating a one-man oversight committee
jokes.explanation: Excellent Jim moment - using bureaucratic absurdity to mock the petty office politics while maintaining control

jokes.text: I've determined this committee is valid.
jokes.setup: Jim's one-man committee deliberation
jokes.punchline: Immediate ruling in favor of his friends after mock deliberation
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the bureaucratic absurdity with Jim's predictable but funny ruling

jokes.text: Come on, we're going to Asian Hooters.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to cheer himself up with Benihana lunch
jokes.punchline: His crude reduction of Benihana to sexualized restaurant concept
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael crudeness that's more uncomfortable than funny, but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Doctor appointment, car trouble, plantar warts, granddad fought in WWII. Use your head, man. I keep mine in here.
jokes.setup: Ryan avoiding the Benihana trip while Jim gets stuck going
jokes.punchline: Ryan's collection of prepared excuses and smug advice to Jim
jokes.explanation: Good Ryan character moment showing his manipulative intelligence and preparedness

jokes.text: Bros before hoes! Why? Because your bros are always there for you. They have got your back after your ho rips your heart out for no good reason.
jokes.setup: Michael's heartbreak philosophy at Benihana
jokes.punchline: Taking the crude motto way too seriously with personal application
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate philosophy lesson that's more cringey than clever

jokes.text: Then suddenly, she's not your ho no mo'.
jokes.setup: Michael's continued 'bros before hoes' philosophy
jokes.punchline: The wordplay ending that he's proud of
jokes.explanation: Weak pun that Michael thinks is clever, fits his character but isn't particularly funny

jokes.text: It is my job to be there for Michael. How can I be there for Michael if I'm here for Michael?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to help Michael at Benihana but can't hear him
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confusing logic about physical proximity vs. emotional support
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight pseudo-philosophical confusion that sounds deep but makes no sense

jokes.text: One part eggnog. Three parts sake! Some places won't make it for you, though, because eggnog is seasonal.
jokes.setup: Michael ordering drinks at Benihana
jokes.punchline: His made-up cocktail recipe and reasoning about eggnog availability
jokes.explanation: Michael's fake expertise about cocktails he's clearly making up on the spot

jokes.text: I miss the days when there was only one party I didn't want to go to.
jokes.setup: Stanley caught between two competing Christmas parties
jokes.punchline: His weary observation about the multiplication of unwanted social obligations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley line - captures his antisocial nature while commenting on the absurd office politics

jokes.text: Actually, the usuba's the better knife when you're working with this quantity.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to show off knife knowledge to Benihana chef
jokes.punchline: Chef's professional correction of Michael's fake expertise
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Michael's fake expertise by actual professional knowledge

jokes.text: I bet you break up with a guy every hour.
jokes.setup: Michael talking to the waitress about his heartbreak
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate assumption about her romantic life as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Michael moment that's more cringey than funny, but fits character

jokes.text: About... your paychecks. Um... your paychecks will be arriving as scheduled on Friday. And they will be in the correct amount that they normally are in.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming to have important announcement to undermine other party
jokes.punchline: The completely mundane paycheck information delivered as if it's news
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Michael's desperation to compete for attention with non-news

jokes.text: Double fudge... Angela.
jokes.setup: Kevin weighing the pros and cons of which party to attend
jokes.punchline: Kevin's simple food-focused decision making process with Angela as the negative
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - reducing complex social situation to food vs. annoying person

jokes.text: My sister and I used to be best friends, and we haven't talked in 16 years over some disagreement I don't even remember.
jokes.setup: Pam talking about not backing down from fights
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation of a decades-long family estrangement over nothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark character reveal showing Pam's hidden stubborn streak with casual devastating detail

jokes.text: Hold its neck back, insert the knife beneath the jaw, bring it all the way around. There's gonna be a good amount of blood.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to help drunk waitress who seems to be asking about something
jokes.punchline: Dwight assuming she wants to know how to butcher a goose and giving detailed instructions
jokes.explanation: Great callback to opening with Dwight's characteristic assumption that everyone needs farming knowledge

jokes.text: They have been checking us out all night. I am not kidding!
jokes.setup: Michael convinced he and Jim are attracting the waitresses
jokes.punchline: His delusional confidence about their romantic success
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional romantic confidence leading to awkward situations

jokes.text: That's what she said? Hello!
jokes.setup: Someone uses phrase that could trigger Michael's catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Michael's confusion when no one responds to his signature joke setup
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment about Michael's signature joke not landing in new environment

jokes.text: I don't walk into your house and steal your Hello Kitty backpack.
jokes.setup: Michael confronting waitress taking something
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific example of hypothetical theft
jokes.explanation: Funny specific detail that reveals Michael's assumptions about young women's possessions

jokes.text: You don't know? Dude, you should know. Yeah, well, it's been hard. They're wearing the exact same uniform. And I've been drinking. And you know how all... waitresses look alike.
jokes.setup: Michael can't identify which waitress is his 'girlfriend'
jokes.punchline: Michael's increasingly problematic excuses including near-racist statement
jokes.explanation: Michael's escalating problematic justifications for his shallow behavior

jokes.text: I honestly don't see what the big deal is. Stevie Wonder is married. Are you going to tell me that Steve Wonder doesn't love his wife just because he's not sure what she looks like?
jokes.setup: Michael defending not knowing which waitress he's dating
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate comparison to Stevie Wonder's blindness
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael logic - using disabled celebrity to justify his own shallow behavior in completely inappropriate way

jokes.text: I marked her arm.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining how he planned to identify the right waitress
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation that he marked her like livestock
jokes.explanation: Shocking reveal that elevates the cringe to new levels - treating a human being like property with casual admission

jokes.text: Or as my ex might say, 'Domo arigato, Mr. Scott-o.'
jokes.setup: Michael's talking head about his rebound relationship
jokes.punchline: Casual racist impression of how Asian person would say his name
jokes.explanation: Problematic joke that's more uncomfortable than funny, though fits Michael's character flaws

jokes.text: You have been compromised. Abort mission. Destroy phone.
jokes.setup: Dwight receiving final CIA message on phone
jokes.punchline: Following the instruction literally and destroying his own phone
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the CIA prank with Dwight's literal following of instructions resulting in self-destruction
